description_long: This object consists of a large, rectangular base with a geometric, grid-like surface pattern, supporting two distinct clusters of solar panels. The solar panels are mounted on raised platforms at opposite ends of the base, each array tilted at an angle to maximize sunlight exposure. The panels themselves are dark, likely composed of photovoltaic cells, and are arranged in compact, rectangular groupings. The base appears to be constructed from a metallic or composite material, featuring a repeating triangular or diamond texture. The overall structure is expansive and low-profile, with the solar arrays elevated above the patterned base. This infrastructure is designed for efficient solar energy capture in an urban or industrial setting.
description: A large, patterned metallic base supports two elevated clusters of tilted solar panels, arranged for optimal sunlight capture.
description_view_0: From this view, the two raised solar panel clusters and the geometric pattern of the base are clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the tilt and elevation of the solar panel arrays, as well as the depth of the patterned base.
description_view_2: Seen from above, the full length of the base and the spatial separation between the two solar panel clusters are apparent.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the angular arrangement and elevation of the solar panels relative to the textured base.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30-35 degrees, facing a consistent direction, with a fixed orientation.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on raised platforms with metallic (likely aluminum or steel) support frames.
attribute_array_configuration: Two separate clusters of solar panels, each arranged in a compact rectangular grid of approximately 3x3 panels.
